AI-300|T2|Q1.rationale: Standardizing ML asset management starts with a shared Azure Machine Learning workspace - it is the top-level container that centrally manages data, models, environments and compute. Registries (A) are for cross-workspace sharing and come later.

AI-300|T2|Q2.rationale: Managed compute targets with autoscaling isolate workloads while staying cost-aware: nodes scale up on demand and back to zero when idle, avoiding the contention of a shared cluster (A) and the waste of a fixed-size (B) or always-dedicated (C) cluster.

AI-300|T2|Q4.rationale: Azure Machine Learning compute clusters support low-priority (spot) VMs at a discount. ACI and AKS do not offer the low-priority/spot pricing tier, and local deployment has no Azure discount.

AI-300|T2|Q5.rationale: For a safe, gradual rollout you split traffic between the existing and new deployments behind one online endpoint (blue/green / canary). Replacing or routing 100% (C, D) removes the staged rollback ability; batch (A) is not for live traffic.

AI-300|T2|Q6.rationale: Azure OpenAI fine-tuning and most generative training datasets require JSONL (one JSON object per line). CSV/TSV/plain JSON are not the expected format.

AI-300|T2|Q7.rationale: UTF-8 is the required/standard encoding for Azure AI training and JSONL data files.

AI-300|T2|Q8.rationale: Before fine-tuning you must prepare and upload the training data as a JSONL file of prompt-completion (or messages) pairs. The other steps are not the prerequisite first action.

AI-300|T2|Q9.rationale: To evaluate prompt variants with consistent inputs and no live traffic, first generate synthetic interaction/test data to use as a controlled evaluation dataset.

AI-300|T2|Q11.rationale: To make an asset reusable and governable across workspaces/projects, publish it to an Azure Machine Learning registry. Git sync, pipeline components and environments do not provide the cross-workspace asset sharing a registry does.

AI-300|T2|Q12.rationale: Private endpoints restrict access to Azure ML / dependent resources over a private IP and remove public exposure - the standard way to meet 'no public network access'. NSGs/firewall/service endpoints are weaker, subnet-level controls.

AI-300|T2|Q13.rationale: Registering the Model asset makes the trained artifact versioned, reusable and governed across projects. Components, environments and pipelines serve other purposes.

AI-300|T2|Q16.rationale: No. 'python train.py --training_data training_data' runs the wrong script (train.py vs script.py) and passes the literal string 'training_data' instead of binding the job input. It does not pass the dataset path.

AI-300|T2|Q17.rationale: No. The script reads an argument named 'training_data', but the command supplies '--trainingdata' (missing underscore), so the argument name does not match. Community also voted B.

AI-300|T2|Q18.rationale: No. 'python script.py dataset1.csv' hard-codes the file as a positional value and does not bind it to the job input '${{inputs.training_data}}', so it does not pass the parameter correctly for a command job.

AI-300|T3|Q1.rationale: MLflow experiment tracking is the built-in way to record and compare metrics/params/artifacts across Azure ML training runs. Job logs, App Insights and Monitor alerts are not experiment-comparison tools.

AI-300|T3|Q3.rationale: fairlearn.postprocessing.ThresholdOptimizer adjusts decision thresholds on an already-trained model to satisfy fairness constraints without retraining - the typical post-hoc mitigation.

AI-300|T3|Q9.rationale: When a new deployment is unhealthy, the first mitigation is to roll traffic back to the previous known-good deployment to restore service, then investigate.

AI-300|T3|Q13.rationale: Archive the model version to retire it from active use while preserving lineage/auditability. Deleting or unregistering destroys history.

AI-300|T3|Q14.rationale: Traffic splitting between deployments on the online endpoint enables safe staged rollout/comparison of a new model version.

AI-300|T4|Q3.rationale: Configure a managed virtual network for the Foundry resource (A) and disable public network access (D) to lock it down. API keys (B) violate least-privilege/no-secrets; a separate subscription (C) and disabling all inbound (E) are not the required network-isolation steps. Matches community AD.

AI-300|T4|Q7.rationale: Store prompts as versioned files in Git for change history, review and rollback - prompt assets are code-like and benefit from source control.

AI-300|T4|Q8.rationale: Data Zone Standard gives low-latency, real-time (non-batch) interactive throughput within a data zone for many applications. Batch types add latency; Developer is for limited dev use.

AI-300|T4|Q9.rationale: Use Entra ID RBAC with least privilege: assign Azure AI Developer to developers (C) and an appropriate admin role where needed (A). Disabling Entra auth (B) and sharing one API key (D) violate the security requirements.

AI-300|T4|Q10.rationale: gpt-4o is the multimodal Azure OpenAI model that accepts both text and images and supports fine-tuning. davinci-002, gpt-35-turbo and gpt-4 (base) do not fit the text+image fine-tune requirement.

AI-300|T4|Q11.rationale: Yes. The Evaluation experience with prompt variants captures inputs, outputs, token usage and latency for comparison, meeting the goal. Community voted A.

AI-300|T4|Q12.rationale: Yes. Enabling tracing via the prompt flow SDK before runs captures inputs, outputs, token usage and latencies - a valid solution to the same goal.

AI-300|T4|Q13.rationale: Yes. Turning on Tracing for the project's prompt flow in Foundry and running tests produces the required trace data (inputs/outputs/tokens/latency). NOTE: community voted B for this variant - verify, as wording differences in this Yes/No series matter.

AI-300|T5|Q3.rationale: Groundedness measures whether a generated answer is supported by the provided source/context - the evaluator for RAG factual grounding. Coherence/fluency/similarity assess other qualities.

AI-300|T5|Q4.rationale: RelevanceEvaluator scores how relevant the response is to the question/context. Use it when relevance is the target metric.

AI-300|T5|Q6.rationale: Implement an evaluation step in CI (GitHub Actions) so prompts/models are automatically evaluated before deployment - the repeatable, automated gate. Manual review and latency monitoring are not automated quality gates.

AI-300|T5|Q7.rationale: For a RAG solution, configure the Groundedness evaluator first to ensure answers are supported by retrieved content. Protected material/indirect attacks/content safety address different risks.

AI-300|T6|Q1.rationale: Choose a model fit for purpose: conversational-reasoning model for an interactive assistant (A), multimodal when combining text+image (D), and one with enterprise governance for regulated data (E). 'Largest' (B) and 'smallest' (C) as blanket rules are wrong. Matches community ADE.

AI-300|T6|Q7.rationale: To improve RAG retrieval quality, tune chunk size/overlap to the content (A) and add an optimized re-ranker (B). Raising token limits (C) and changing embedding length (D) do not directly fix retrieval relevance.

AI-103|T1|Q2.rationale: PII Detection prevents the agent from revealing customer information even if a document with customer data is added by mistake. Prompt shields target injection; content filters target harmful categories. Community voted C.

AI-103|T1|Q3.rationale: To let the agent use the product sheets, add a connection to the Azure AI Search resource in the project so the agent can retrieve from the index.

AI-103|T1|Q9.rationale: When multiple agents must collaborate on one task, use a multi-agent group chat session so agents coordinate within the orchestration rather than ad-hoc app code.

AI-103|T1|Q12.rationale: When a custom speech model expires, recognition requests fall back to the most recent base model for the same locale; they do not error out or auto-delete the model.

AI-103|T1|Q13.rationale: To call Azure OpenAI models, assign Cognitive Services OpenAI User (least-privilege data-plane access for inference). Contributor is over-privileged; the others do not grant OpenAI inference.

AI-103|T1|Q14.rationale: To require the agent to use an MCP tool, set tool_choice to the mcp type. 'auto' leaves it optional; 'required' is not the MCP-specific binding; knowledge_base is not the MCP tool type.

AI-103|T1|Q16.rationale: Because API keys are not allowed and Entra auth is required, configure a Bearer token security scheme in the OpenAPI spec so the tool authenticates with an Entra token.

AI-103|T1|Q17.rationale: A model cascade routes easy requests to a smaller/cheaper model and escalates only hard ones to the capable model, balancing cost and quality. Routing all to one model wastes cost or quality.

AI-103|T1|Q19.rationale: To judge output quality/correctness, use evaluation metrics. Latency, run success rate and token usage are operational, not quality, signals.

AI-103|T1|Q21.rationale: Connect the tool to the existing Connection1 so it uses the configured, secured connection rather than manual API keys or caller identity passthrough.

AI-103|T1|Q22.rationale: Tracing gives the per-step execution detail needed to diagnose where/why an agent run behaves unexpectedly. Token usage/monitoring/safety metrics are aggregate signals.

AI-103|T1|Q23.rationale: No. A prompt shield for user prompts guards text prompt injection, not hidden instructions embedded inside uploaded images. It does not mitigate the image-based risk.

AI-103|T1|Q24.rationale: Yes. Image moderation blocks unsafe images before processing, which directly mitigates the unsafe-image upload risk in this scenario.

AI-103|T1|Q25.rationale: No. Prompt shields for documents target injected instructions in grounding documents/text, not malicious instructions hidden in uploaded images.

AI-103|T1|Q26.rationale: No. Protected material detection flags copyrighted content, not unsafe images or hidden image-borne instructions, so it does not meet the goal.

AI-103|T1|Q27.rationale: Use a groundedness evaluator to detect responses not supported by retrieved content (hallucinations). A RAG evaluator is broader; guardrails/fine-tuning are not the measurement asked for.

AI-103|T1|Q28.rationale: Modify the system message instructions to constrain behavior/scope - the most direct, reliable control. Few-shot, top-p and temperature do not enforce scope as deterministically.

AI-103|T1|Q29.rationale: A multimodal model is needed to analyze invoices by combining visual layout and textual content. An SLM/LLM (text-only) or key-phrase model cannot evaluate the visual layout.

AI-103|T1|Q31.rationale: Configure one Azure AI Search connection in Project1 and reference it from each application - centralized, governed, least-privilege. Per-app custom HTTP/direct calls duplicate config and weaken governance.

AI-103|T1|Q34.rationale: Persist conversation context by storing the conversation/thread ID and supplying it on subsequent requests, so the service retains state across turns.

AI-103|T1|Q36.rationale: Agentic RAG lets the agent plan and run multiple targeted retrievals/reasoning steps for complex queries, improving completeness over a single classic RAG pass.

AI-103|T1|Q38.rationale: Switch to Retrieval Augmented Generation so responses are grounded in source content, improving completeness/accuracy. Token/model tweaks do not add the missing grounded content.

AI-103|T1|Q39.rationale: For throttling/429 errors, implement a retry policy with exponential backoff and jitter. New threads, fewer tools or smaller files do not address rate limiting.

AI-103|T1|Q41.rationale: No. Increasing max_tokens only raises the output length cap; it does not make the model include required clauses that it is already omitting.

AI-103|T1|Q42.rationale: Yes. A reflection pass that regenerates the response when required clauses are missing directly improves completeness.

AI-103|T1|Q43.rationale: No. Increasing temperature adds randomness; it does not improve completeness and may worsen omission of required clauses.

AI-103|T1|Q44.rationale: Yes. An evaluation flow that scores completeness and blocks responses below a threshold enforces complete responses, meeting the goal.

AI-103|T1|Q45.rationale: Background removal that preserves the subject is best done with mask inpainting: supply the image plus a mask marking the region to change. Full image_to_image regeneration risks altering the subject.

AI-103|T1|Q46.rationale: To remove a specific logo while keeping the rest, apply mask-based inpainting to the logo region. Prompt/seed/guidance changes regenerate the whole image unpredictably.

AI-103|T1|Q47.rationale: prebuilt-layout extracts tables, structure and layout from documents with varied layouts - needed for invoices. prebuilt-read is OCR-only; the others are not layout extractors.

AI-103|T1|Q48.rationale: Set input_fidelity to high to preserve fine detail/text from the input image during image editing/generation.

AI-103|T1|Q50.rationale: To screen text embedded in images, run OCR (Azure Vision) and apply keyword scanning over the OCR output. Prompt shields/blocklists/image moderation do not scan extracted in-image text the same way.

AI-103|T1|Q51.rationale: Prompt shields for documents protect against injected instructions hidden in ingested documents/grounding content.

AI-103|T1|Q53.rationale: Azure Content Understanding extracts structured fields from mixed-layout documents (tables, logos, varied layouts) - suited to invoice processing.

AI-103|T1|Q56.rationale: Azure Content Understanding handles multi-format/multimodal extraction for varied invoice layouts. Document Intelligence/Image Analysis/chat completions are narrower.

AI-103|T1|Q58.rationale: For large volumes of recorded audio files, run a batch transcription job - the scalable, asynchronous option. Real-time is for live streams.

AI-103|T1|Q59.rationale: For a live voice agent, use real-time speech to text for incoming audio and text to speech for responses - the standard low-latency conversational pattern.

AI-103|T1|Q60.rationale: Split mixed-language segments into single-language segments and translate each separately so Translator handles each correctly. Treating mixed text as one document/source language degrades quality.

AI-103|T1|Q62.rationale: Azure AI Search provides the index/retrieval layer for grounding the agent on the documents.

AI-103|T1|Q63.rationale: Configure content=markdown so the analyzer returns structured markdown (preserving tables/headings) for downstream processing.

AI-103|T1|Q64.rationale: Use a custom Azure Content Understanding analyzer that defines the required invoice fields as the extraction schema - tailored field extraction across varied layouts. Prebuilt-layout/documentSearch are generic.

AI-103|T1|Q65.rationale: When responses are incomplete/ungrounded, review groundedness evaluation metrics to see whether outputs are supported by retrieved content. Indexer status/latency/drift address other failure modes.
